title: cancel+disconnect leaves payer/payee state inconsistent
description: In /cancel: send_payment_message fires, then we break on channel-closed without persisting Expired. The peer received Cancelled and moved to Expired; we reconnect with the session still in Requested/AddressProvided. resync_sessions then re-broadcasts Request/Address. The peer (in Expired state) hits the catch-all in dispatch_payment and does nothing. Payer is permanently stuck with a live session whose counterpart is dead. Trigger: connection drop between send and state-persist, or MLS encryption failure (returns true but does not send — Cancelled silently dropped while state is never updated).
close_reason: Fixed: moved upsert_session(Expired) before send_payment_message in /cancel handler. Local state is now persisted before the network send, so a channel-close break leaves consistent Expired state on both sides.
